notes: Boron carbide (B4C) armor ceramic. Density 2.52 g/cm³ — lightest armor ceramic. Knoop hardness ~3700 HK (30 GPa) — hardest ceramic. RHAe ~4.0-5.0 per mm vs KE standalone tile. Used in M1A2 SEP v2 turret behind DU inserts. Highest areal density efficiency but most expensive. Degrades under extremely high pressure (shock-induced amorphization at ~25 GPa). Fracture toughness K1c ~2.5-3.5 MPa·m^½.

source.reference: Ceramic armor manufacturer datasheets (CoorsTek, Ceradyne), ARL research
source.methodology: RHAe from published ballistic test data on hot-pressed B4C tiles vs AP, normalized to RHA. Knoop hardness and fracture toughness from ceramics literature.
